footer.description: Bringing the ancient art of tea ceremony to Wellington. Experience tranquility, mindfulness, and the beauty of tea culture.

hero.title: Experience the Art of Tea
hero.description: Discover the ancient traditions of tea ceremony in Wellington, where every cup tells a story of mindfulness and connection.

intro.title: Why Moso Tea
intro.description: We bring the ancient art of tea ceremony to New Zealand, creating moments of peace and connection in our carefully curated space.
intro.feature1Description: Rooted in centuries of tradition, we bring the art and philosophy of tea ceremony to life — one mindful cup at a time.
intro.feature2Description: Nestled in the hills of Wainuiomata, our farm studio is a place where time slows down and the outside world quietly falls away.
intro.feature3Description: From the first scent of leaves to the last sip, every session is crafted to be unhurried, intimate, and deeply personal.

experiences.title: Our Experience
experiences.subtitle: A single, unhurried journey through the world of tea — guided, hands-on, and deeply personal.

teaJourney.title: Tea Journey
teaJourney.description: Learn the story of tea — its culture, its trees, and the art of brewing — guided by your host.

handsOn.title: Hands-on Experience
handsOn.description: Pick, process, brew, and share — experience the full journey of tea with your own hands.

testimonials.title: Guest Reflections
testimonials.quote1: A truly transformative experience. The attention to detail and the peaceful atmosphere made me feel like I was transported to another world.
testimonials.quote2: The perfect escape from city life. I've attended three sessions now and each one brings new insights and a sense of calm.
testimonials.quote3: An authentic and beautifully curated tea ceremony. The tea master's knowledge and gentle guidance made it accessible for beginners like me.

hero.title: Our Story
hero.subtitle: Sharing the art of tea since 2021

story.title: A Journey of Tea
story.p1: Moso Tea was born from a simple yet profound love for the art of tea ceremony. Founded in 2021, our studio has become a sanctuary for those seeking moments of stillness in Wainuiomata, Wellington.
story.p2: The name "Moso" carries two quiet meanings. In Taiwanese, moso means slow — an invitation to pause, to breathe, and to be present. It is also the name of the moso bamboo, a plant we deeply admire not for its dramatic growth, but for its stillness and resilience. The bamboo stands quietly through all seasons, rooted and unhurried. Our tea trees came to us by chance, much like the bamboo — an unexpected gift that found its way into our lives and shaped everything that followed.
story.p3: What began as a small gathering of tea enthusiasts has grown into a beloved space where people from all walks of life come together to experience the meditative beauty of tea culture.
story.p4: Our lifestyle farm studio in Wainuiomata offers a rare opportunity to step away from the pace of everyday life and immerse yourself in an ancient tradition — one that celebrates the simple, unhurried act of preparing and sharing tea.

philosophy.quote: In the simple act of making tea, we find a path to presence. Each cup is an invitation to slow down, to breathe, and to connect with the moment.
philosophy.quoteZh: 在泡茶的簡單動作中，我們找到了通往當下的道路。每一杯茶都是一份邀請，讓我們放慢腳步，深呼吸，與當下連結。

expect.title: What to Expect
expect.item1Description: Our certified tea masters bring years of training and deep cultural knowledge to every session.
expect.item2Description: A quiet, peaceful space where you can slow down, focus on the moment, and enjoy an unhurried tea experience.
expect.item3Description: Premium teas, traditional utensils, and light refreshments are included in every experience.
expect.item4Description: Our sessions are designed to give you time to fully absorb and appreciate each moment.

founder1.name: Joyce Peng
founder1.description: Joyce is the heart of every ceremony at Moso Tea. Originally from Taiwan, she brings a lifetime of tea culture into each session — her warmth and gentle guidance make every guest feel at home from the very first cup.

founder2.name: Jenson Lin
founder2.description: Jenson tends to the tea garden and oversees the making of every leaf. His quiet dedication to the land and the craft is what gives Moso Tea its roots — quite literally.

founder3.name: Liyin Lin
founder3.description: Liyin grew up surrounded by tea. Now she shares that world with guests in her own words, bridging cultures and making the art of tea feel accessible, engaging, and alive.

cta.title: Ready to Experience Tea?
cta.description: Join us for an unforgettable journey into the art of tea ceremony. Book your experience today and discover the tranquility that awaits.

hero.title: The Workshop
hero.description: One experience. Two parts. A complete journey through the world of tea.

items.1: The story and philosophy behind tea culture
items.2: Understanding the tea tree — varieties, seasons, and terroir
items.3: The art of brewing — water temperature, timing, and technique

items.1: Walk the tea garden and hand-pick your own leaves
items.2: Learn the traditional process of making tea from scratch
items.3: Brew and taste your own creation
items.4: Share the experience with your group over a final tea ceremony

experiences.stats: 90 minutes  ·  Max 8 guests  ·  NZD $75 per person

included.title: What's Included
included.item1Description: Your host leads every step, sharing knowledge built over years of practice.
included.item2Description: Step into our tea garden, learn to read the leaves, and pick them by hand.
included.item3Description: Transform your freshly picked leaves into finished tea, guided by tradition.
included.item4Description: Everything you need for the ceremony is prepared and waiting for you.

faq.title: Common Questions
faq.q1: Do I need any prior experience with tea ceremonies?
faq.a1: Not at all! Our experience is designed for all levels, from complete beginners to seasoned tea enthusiasts. Your host will guide you through every step, ensuring you feel comfortable and engaged throughout the session.
faq.a2: We recommend comfortable, modest clothing in muted colours. You'll be seated on cushions on the floor, so loose-fitting trousers or a long skirt work well. Please remove your shoes upon entering the tea room. Since our experience includes a walk through the tea garden, we also recommend wearing comfortable closed-toe shoes suitable for light outdoor walking.
faq.q3: Can I book for a special occasion or event?
faq.a3: Absolutely! Our workshop is perfect for birthdays, anniversaries, team gatherings, or any special celebration. Contact us to discuss customised arrangements.
faq.a4: Our main tea room is accessible via a ground-level entrance. We can also arrange seating accommodations for guests who may have difficulty sitting on the floor. Please let us know your requirements when booking.
faq.a5: We offer full refunds for cancellations made at least 24 hours before your scheduled session. Cancellations within 24 hours may be rescheduled subject to availability. Please contact us if you have any concerns about your booking.
faq.q6: Do I need to avoid wearing perfume or cologne?
faq.a6: Yes, we kindly ask all guests to refrain from wearing perfume, cologne, or strongly scented products. The delicate aromas of tea are central to the experience, and strong fragrances can interfere with everyone's enjoyment.
